1	# CampusFit Static Audit
2	
3	## 1. Verdict
4	- Overall conclusion: Fail
5	- Basis: The repository has real product structure, documentation, security primitives, and domain modules, but several explicit prompt requirements are either missing end-to-end or materially weakened by contract, workflow, and authorization defects.
6	
7	## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
8	- Reviewed:
9	  - `README.md`, `.env.example`, `docker-compose.yml`, `contracts/openapi.yaml`
10	  - Vue router, pages, stores, composables, adapters, and selected frontend tests under `apps/web/src`
11	  - Spring controllers, services, entities, security config, migrations, and selected tests under `services/api/src`
12	- Not reviewed:
13	  - Runtime DB contents
14	  - Browser-rendered UI behavior
15	  - External email/SMS/WeCom integrations
16	  - Real file download/upload interoperability
17	- Intentionally not executed:
18	  - Project startup
19	  - Docker / Docker Compose
20	  - Tests
21	  - Browser/manual flows
22	- Manual verification required for:
23	  - Runtime boot and environment wiring
24	  - Actual MySQL partition creation and query performance
25	  - Scheduled cleanup/aggregation timing
26	  - Real browser rendering / responsive behavior
27	  - External notification channels
28	  - Export-file download, decrypt, upload, and import interoperability
29	
30	## 3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
31	- Core prompt: offline-first local deployment with three roles, local auth, fitness assessment/goals/check-ins, study plans tied to academic hierarchy, inbound receiving state machine with discrepancy/supervisor rules, admin master-data import/export/merge/history, unified notification center, protected exports/account deletion, and performance/admin reporting.
32	- Main implementation areas mapped:
33	  - Frontend routes/pages/stores/adapters in `apps/web/src/app` and `apps/web/src/modules/*`
34	  - Backend controllers/services/security/migrations in `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/*` and `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration`
35	  - Static contract/documentation in `contracts/openapi.yaml`, `README.md`, `.env.example`, and `docker-compose.yml`
36	
37	## 4. Section-by-section Review
38	
39	### 1. Hard Gates
40	- `1.1 Documentation and static verifiability`
41	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
42	  - Rationale: Startup/configuration artifacts are present and structurally consistent enough for a reviewer to find entry points, but the shipped OpenAPI contract is materially inconsistent with implemented controllers and real frontend expectations.
43	  - Evidence: `README.md:5`, `README.md:24`, `.env.example:6`, `.env.example:28`, `docker-compose.yml:3`, `services/api/pom.xml:26`, `apps/web/package.json:6`, `apps/web/src/app/router.ts:4`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:241`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:1222`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:1249`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/controller/CheckInController.java:34`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/controller/NotificationController.java:50`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:86`
44	  - Manual verification note: Runtime startup still requires manual verification because no commands were executed.
45	- `1.2 Whether the delivered project materially deviates from the Prompt`
46	  - Conclusion: Fail
47	  - Rationale: Several explicit prompt commitments are not met end-to-end: study plans are not tied to the full academic hierarchy, supervisor review is not privilege-separated, dashboard data contracts do not match, and encrypted export files do not have a matching import flow.
48	  - Evidence: `apps/web/src/modules/study/components/PlanForm.vue:16`, `apps/web/src/modules/study/types.ts:43`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyPlan.java:27`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:118`, `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V2__seed_roles_permissions.sql:6`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/dto/DashboardResponse.java:18`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/UserDashboard.vue:19`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:221`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:73`
49	
50	### 2. Delivery Completeness
51	- `2.1 Whether all explicitly stated core functional requirements are implemented`
52	  - Conclusion: Fail
53	  - Rationale: Core flows are incomplete or broken in static review: discrepancy retrieval is missing from the delivered API, the dashboard contract is incompatible across layers, cross-account migration from an exported file is absent, and the study-plan hierarchy is only partially modeled.
54	  - Evidence: `apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:171`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:43`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/DashboardService.java:57`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/OpsDashboard.vue:19`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java:53`, `apps/web/src/modules/exports/pages/ExportsPage.vue:64`, `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V4__create_study_tables.sql:5`, `apps/web/src/modules/study/pages/PlansPage.vue:69`
55	- `2.2 Whether the project is a basic 0-to-1 deliverable rather than a partial/demo`
56	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
57	  - Rationale: The repository is a real multi-module application with docs, migrations, routes, security, and tests. Mock behavior is explicitly gated, but several real HTTP paths depend on mock-only assumptions or missing backend support.
58	  - Evidence: `README.md:42`, `apps/web/src/services/adapters/adapter-factory.ts:7`, `apps/web/src/services/adapters/mock-adapter.ts:337`, `apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:166`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/PutawayService.java:29`
59	
60	### 3. Engineering and Architecture Quality
61	- `3.1 Whether the project adopts a reasonable engineering structure and module decomposition`
62	  - Conclusion: Pass
63	  - Rationale: Frontend and backend responsibilities are separated by domain and role; security, reporting, export, inbound, study, and fitness concerns are not collapsed into a single file.
64	  - Evidence: `README.md:5`, `apps/web/src/app/router.ts:19`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:21`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/DashboardService.java:22`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataImportService.java:30`
65	- `3.2 Whether the project shows maintainability and extensibility`
66	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
67	  - Rationale: DTO/service layering and targeted policies exist, but repeated contract drift between frontend/backend/OpenAPI and repeated in-memory pagination patterns increase change risk.
68	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/policy/ReferentialIntegrityPolicy.java:9`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/policy/GoalRecalculationPolicy.java:18`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:51`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/controller/StudyPlanController.java:36`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:276`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/controller/CheckInController.java:34`
69	
70	### 4. Engineering Details and Professionalism
71	- `4.1 Whether the engineering details reflect professional software practice`
72	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
73	  - Rationale: The codebase has centralized exception handling, input validation, RBAC, AES-based converters for fitness metrics, caching, and scheduled maintenance. That said, key API contracts drift, supervisor review is under-protected, account exports are not masked by default, and account deletion semantics do not match the stated requirement.
74	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java:22`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/dto/AssessmentRequest.java:18`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/encryption/EncryptionService.java:14`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:48`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:188`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionService.java:80`
75	- `4.2 Whether the project is organized like a real product or service`
76	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
77	  - Rationale: The application has role-targeted pages, admin tooling, scheduled maintenance, and domain services, but the current test/mocking strategy allows severe real-integration defects to remain undetected.
78	  - Evidence: `apps/web/src/modules/admin/pages/PerformancePage.vue:15`, `apps/web/src/modules/master-data/pages/MergePage.vue:37`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/CampusFitApplication.java:7`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/FileRetentionService.java:32`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/__tests__/DashboardPage.spec.ts:20`, `apps/web/src/modules/operations/__tests__/ReceivingDetailPage.spec.ts:16`
79	
80	### 5. Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit
81	- `5.1 Whether the project accurately understands and responds to the business goal and constraints`
82	  - Conclusion: Fail
83	  - Rationale: The repository understands the broad product shape, but several prompt semantics are weakened: supervisor review is not a distinct approval boundary, migration is not file-based end-to-end, and the study hierarchy is reduced to optional term/course IDs with no school/major/class linkage in the user flow.
84	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V2__seed_roles_permissions.sql:6`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:118`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:73`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:221`, `apps/web/src/modules/study/components/PlanForm.vue:35`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/dto/StudyPlanRequest.java:15`
85	
86	### 6. Aesthetics
87	- `6.1 Whether the visual and interaction design fits the scenario`
88	  - Conclusion: Cannot Confirm Statistically
89	  - Rationale: The source contains responsive layouts, card-based grouping, visual hierarchy, and interaction styling, but actual rendering quality, alignment, spacing, and feedback were not executed in a browser.
90	  - Evidence: `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/UserDashboard.vue:14`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/AdminDashboard.vue:13`, `apps/web/src/modules/admin/pages/PerformancePage.vue:68`, `apps/web/src/modules/master-data/pages/MasterDataPage.vue:68`
91	  - Manual verification note: Open the main role pages, notification center, receiving workspace, and admin pages in a browser to confirm layout, readability, responsiveness, and hover/click states.
92	
93	## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)
94	
95	1. Severity: Blocker
96	   Title: Cross-account migration from an exported file is not implemented end-to-end
97	   Conclusion: Fail
98	   Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:221`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:225`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:73`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportService.java:53`, `apps/web/src/modules/exports/pages/ExportsPage.vue:64`, `apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:285`
99	   Impact: The delivered export flow writes password-encrypted `.enc` files, but the import path accepts raw JSON maps rather than uploaded/exported files, and there is no user-facing import flow. The prompt's cross-account migration requirement is therefore absent in practice.
100	   Minimum actionable fix: Add a mching file-upload/decrypt/import path for exported account files and expose it in the exports UI; verify the exported artifact can round-trip through import without manual JSON extraction.
101	
102	2. Severity: High
103	   Title: Discrepancy retrieval required by the web client is missing from the delivered API
104	   Conclusion: Fail
105	   Evidence: `apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts:171`, `apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts:56`, `apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts:12`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:43`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:570`
106	   Impact: Operations pages depend on `GET /api/inbound/receipts/{id}/discrepancies`, but no such controller route or contract path exists. Real discrepancy pages cannot load against the delivered backend.
107	   Minimum actionable fix: Implement and document a discrepancy list endpoint, or refactor the frontend to use an existing backend path and add controller tests for it.
108	
109	3. Severity: High
110	   Title: PUTAWAY task reads are implemented as task creation and can duplicate tasks
111	   Conclusion: Fail
112	   Evidence: `apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts:103`, `apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts:13`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:108`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/PutawayService.java:29`
113	   Impact: The frontend treats `POST /putaway` without `taskId` as a read operation, but the backend generates and saves new tasks on that path. Simple page refreshes or background loads can multiply pending tasks and corrupt workflow state.
114	   Minimum actionable fix: Separate read and create/complete semantics. Add a dedicated `GET /putaway` endpoint and make task generation idempotent.
115	
116	4. Severity: High
117	   Title: Dashboard frontend and backend data contracts are incompatible
118	   Conclusion: Fail
119	   Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/dto/DashboardResponse.java:18`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/DashboardService.java:57`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/store.ts:15`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/UserDashboard.vue:19`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/OpsDashboard.vue:19`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/components/AdminDashboard.vue:18`
120	   Impact: The central landing experience depends on keys such as `fitnessGoals`, `pendingDiscrepancies`, `recentActivity`, and `charts.*`, but the backend returns only `userRole`, `metrics`, and a different `summary` shape. Real dashboards will not render the required information.
121	   Minimum actionable fix: Define one shared dashboard schema, update backend responses and OpenAPI to match it, and add integration tests for each role's dashboard payload.
122	
123	5. Severity: High
124	   Title: Study plans are not bound to the required academic hierarchy
125	   Conclusion: Fail
126	   Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/entity/StudyPlan.java:27`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/dto/StudyPlanRequest.java:15`, `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V4__create_study_tables.sql:5`, `apps/web/src/modules/study/components/PlanForm.vue:16`, `apps/web/src/modules/study/types.ts:43`
127	   Impact: The prompt requires plans tied to term, school, major, class, and course. The current implementation exposes only optional `termId` and `courseId`, and the UI does not let users choose any academic hierarchy at creation time.
128	   Minimum actionable fix: Extend the model and UI to select and validate the required hierarchy, and enforce referential integrity server-side.
129	
130	6. Severity: High
131	   Title: Supervisor review is not privilege-separated from normal operations staff
132	   Conclusion: Fail
133	   Evidence: `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V2__seed_roles_permissions.sql:6`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:53`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:118`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyService.java:33`
134	   Impact: Threshold discrepancies are marked as requiring supervisor approval, but the only relevant roles are `OPERATIONS_STAFF` and `ADMIN`, and the review endpoint explicitly allows ordinary operations staff. The same role that creates the discrepancy can self-approve it.
135	   Minimum actionable fix: Introduce a distinct supervisor/manager authority or restrict supervisor review to admin/supervisor-only users and enforce separation-of-duty rules.
136	
137	7. Severity: High
138	   Title: Account exports include raw profile identifiers despite the masked-by-default export requirement
139	   Conclusion: Fail
140	   Evidence: `README.md:105`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:156`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:188`
141	   Impact: `ACCOUNT_DATA` exports include raw username, email, and phone. That directly conflicts with the stated "masked in logs and exports by default" requirement.
142	   Minimum actionable fix: Mask or omit profile identifiers by default in export payloads, and require an explicit opt-in path if reversible export of identifiers is needed.
143	
144	8. Severity: High
145	   Title: Account deletion anonymizes the user row instead of hard-deleting identifiers
146	   Conclusion: Fail
147	   Evidence: `README.md:107`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionService.java:74`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionServiceTest.java:152`
148	   Impact: The prompt requires hard-deleting identifiers while retaining de-identified audit events. The implementation keeps the user row, replaces username with `anon_*`, and sets status `DELETED`; the shipped test explicitly validates that non-deletion behavior.
149	   Minimum actionable fix: Remove or fully detach identifying user records while preserving separate de-identified audit artifacts; update tests to assert the prompt-compliant deletion semantics.
150	
151	9. Severity: High
152	   Title: The published OpenAPI contract materially diverges from implementation
153	   Conclusion: Fail
154	   Evidence: `contracts/openapi.yaml:276`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/controller/CheckInController.java:34`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:1222`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/controller/NotificationController.java:50`, `contracts/openapi.yaml:1249`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:39`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:86`
155	   Impact: Static reviewers and generated clients cannot trust the contract. Examples include paginated check-ins vs list responses, notification status schema mismatches, `201` vs documented `200`, and implemented routes that are absent from the contract.
156	   Minimum actionable fix: Reconcile controllers, frontend expectations, and OpenAPI from a single source of truth, then add contract tests.
157	
158	10. Severity: Medium
159	    Title: Large list endpoints paginate in memory after loading all rows
160	    Conclusion: Partial Fail
161	    Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:51`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/controller/StudyPlanController.java:36`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/controller/TermController.java:36`
162	    Impact: The prompt calls for high-performance querying with pagination defaults of 25 rows. Current controllers fetch entire lists and slice them in memory, which will not scale with real data volumes.
163	    Minimum actionable fix: Move pagination, sorting, and filtering to repository/database queries and add tests around page boundaries.
164	
165	11. Severity: Medium
166	    Title: Performance/partitioning requirements are only partially implemented
167	    Conclusion: Partial Fail
168	    Evidence: `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V3__create_fitness_tables.sql:84`, `services/api/src/main/resources/db/migration/V4__create_study_tables.sql:5`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/AggregationService.java:41`, `services/api/src/main/resources/application.yml:28`
169	    Impact: There is month-based partitioning for `t_check_in`, nightly aggregation, and a 5-minute cache TTL, but no static evidence of the promised term-based partitioning and only limited pre-aggregated metrics.
170	    Minimum actionable fix: Implement and document the missing partitioning strategy and broaden pre-aggregation to the actual dashboard/report workloads.
171	
172	12. Severity: Medium
173	    Title: CSV import parsing is naive and will break valid quoted CSV input
174	    Conclusion: Fail
175	    Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataImportService.java:143`, `services/api/pom.xml:76`
176	    Impact: The import code uses `line.split(",")` despite shipping an OpenCSV dependency. Valid CSV rows containing quoted commas or escaped fields can be misparsed, causing false validation errors or corrupted imports.
177	    Minimum actionable fix: Replace the manual split with a standards-compliant CSV parser and add tests for quoted fields and embedded commas.
178	
179	13. Severity: Medium
180	    Title: Account deletion password confirmation is collected in the UI but ignored by the actual deletion flow
181	    Conclusion: Partial Fail
182	    Evidence: `apps/web/src/modules/exports/components/AccountDeletionDialog.vue:16`, `apps/web/src/modules/exports/pages/ExportsPage.vue:51`, `apps/web/src/modules/exports/store.ts:33`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/controller/ExportController.java:80`
183	    Impact: The UI implies re-authentication, but neither the frontend store nor the backend endpoint consumes the password. A valid session alone is enough to trigger deletion.
184	    Minimum actionable fix: Either remove the misleading password prompt or enforce password re-verification server-side before processing deletion.
185	
186	## 6. Security Review Summary
187	- Authentication entry points
188	  - Conclusion: Pass
189	  - Evidence and reasoning: Local sign-up/sign-in/sign-out/current-user routes exist; passwords are bcrypt-hashed; failed-attempt lockout and 30-minute inactivity timeout are implemented in code and config. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthController.java:28`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/PasswordService.java:12`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthService.java:82`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptService.java:19`, `services/api/src/main/resources/application.yml:45`.
190	- Route-level authorization
191	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
192	  - Evidence and reasoning: Backend route guards exist for `/api/admin/**`, `/api/inbound/**`, and authenticated `/api/**`; frontend routes also carry `requiresAuth` and role metadata. However, the supervisor-review route is broader than the prompt implies. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:48`, `apps/web/src/app/router.ts:169`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:118`.
193	- Object-level authorization
194	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
195	  - Evidence and reasoning: Service-layer ownership checks exist for study plans, goals, check-ins, notifications, and exports. Static implementation is present, but controller-level negative-path coverage is weak. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/service/StudyPlanService.java:40`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/GoalService.java:90`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInService.java:36`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationService.java:104`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:128`.
196	- Function-level authorization
197	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
198	  - Evidence and reasoning: Method-level `@PreAuthorize` is used for admin-only notification creation/status, admin performance, and admin role assignment. The weakness is that supervisor review is not protected by a distinct approval authority. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/controller/NotificationController.java:34`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingController.java:38`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthController.java:60`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/inbound/controller/InboundReceiptController.java:118`.
199	- Tenant / user data isolation
200	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
[result-id: r1]

200	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
201	  - Evidence and reasoning: User-scoped reads and ownership checks are present in notifications, exports, study, and fitness flows, but static test coverage is insufficient to prove all HTTP-level isolation paths. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationService.java:80`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:129`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/study/service/StudyPlanService.java:44`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInService.java:109`.
202	- Admin / internal / debug endpoint protection
203	  - Conclusion: Pass
204	  - Evidence and reasoning: Admin endpoints are behind backend role checks, and the explicitly exposed health endpoint is narrowly scoped. See `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/config/SecurityConfig.java:49`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingController.java:38`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingControllerTest.java:80`.
205	
206	## 7. Tests and Logging Review
207	- Unit tests
208	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
209	  - Rationale: There is meaningful service/policy coverage for auth, fitness, inbound state rules, master-data integrity/merge, notifications, exports, and deletion/import logic.
210	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthServiceTest.java:60`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptServiceTest.java:31`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/fitness/policy/GoalRecalculationPolicyTest.java:37`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachineTest.java:66`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/DuplicateMergeServiceTest.java:52`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationServiceTest.java:51`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportServiceTest.java:68`
211	- API / integration tests
212	  - Conclusion: Fail
213	  - Rationale: Controller-level coverage is limited to auth and reporting. Core HTTP flows for inbound, study, master-data, exports, notifications, and dashboard payload compatibility are largely untested.
214	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthControllerTest.java:44`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingControllerTest.java:35`
215	- Logging categories / observability
216	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
217	  - Rationale: Structured logging exists for exception handling, exports, import jobs, encryption setup, file retention, and performance aggregation. Admin performance reporting is also present.
218	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/exception/GlobalExceptionHandler.java:84`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:99`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/masterdata/service/MasterDataImportService.java:119`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/FileRetentionService.java:45`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/AggregationService.java:41`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/reporting/service/SlowQueryLogService.java:27`
219	- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs / responses
220	  - Conclusion: Partial Pass
221	  - Rationale: User IDs are masked in export logs, and fitness metrics are encrypted at rest, but raw export file paths are logged and other high-risk privacy behavior exists elsewhere (for example unmasked account export content).
222	  - Evidence: `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/encryption/FieldMasker.java:8`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportService.java:99`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/FileRetentionService.java:45`, `services/api/src/main/java/com/campusfit/shared/encryption/EncryptionService.java:14`
223	
224	## 8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)
225	
226	### 8.1 Test Overview
227	- Unit tests exist for backend services/policies across auth, fitness, inbound, master data, notifications, and exports. Evidence: `services/api/pom.xml:104`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthServiceTest.java:35`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachineTest.java:23`
228	- API/controller tests exist, but only for auth and reporting. Evidence: `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthControllerTest.java:44`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingControllerTest.java:35`
229	- Frontend tests exist and use Vitest. Evidence: `apps/web/package.json:10`, `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/__tests__/DashboardPage.spec.ts:1`, `apps/web/src/modules/operations/__tests__/ReceivingDetailPage.spec.ts:1`
230	- Documentation provides test commands, but they were not executed for this audit. Evidence: `README.md:60`, `apps/web/package.json:10`
231	
232	### 8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
233	
234	| Requirement / Risk Point | Mapped Test Case(s) | Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock | Coverage Assessment | Gap | Minimum Test Addition |
235	| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
236	| Local auth, lockout, sign-in/out, session validation | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthControllerTest.java:53`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthServiceTest.java:60`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptServiceTest.java:31` | Hashed password asserted at `AuthServiceTest.java:85`; token issuance at `AuthServiceTest.java:137`; lockout timing at `LoginAttemptServiceTest.java:50` | sufficient | No controller test for admin role assignment | Add controller tests for role assignment and negative authorization cases |
237	| Fitness goal recalculation and audit trail after missed check-ins | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/fitness/policy/GoalRecalculationPolicyTest.java:37`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/fitness/service/CheckInServiceTest.java:81` | Audit record asserted at `GoalRecalculationPolicyTest.java:103`; date/target changes asserted at `GoalRecalculationPolicyTest.java:57` | basically covered | `CheckInServiceTest` does not actually assert the recalculation call/path | Add a service test that proves two missed check-ins trigger `recalculationPolicy.recalculate(...)` |
238	| Inbound state machine and discrepancy thresholds | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/InboundStateMachineTest.java:66`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/inbound/service/DiscrepancyServiceTest.java:58` | Transition guards asserted at `InboundStateMachineTest.java:149`; supervisor-required discrepancy asserted at `DiscrepancyServiceTest.java:133` | basically covered | No controller test for real HTTP workflow, missing discrepancy endpoint, or putaway duplication bug | Add controller/integration tests for receipt detail, discrepancy retrieval, putaway task reads, and supervisor review |
239	| Supervisor review authorization separation | None found | None found | missing | Current tests do not verify that only a distinct supervisor/admin authority can approve threshold discrepancies | Add security tests proving ordinary operations staff cannot perform supervisor review |
240	| Study plans tied to academic hierarchy | None found | None found | missing | No tests assert school/major/class/course linkage because the UI and request model do not implement it | Add frontend and backend tests for hierarchy selection, validation, and persistence |
241	| Export password protection and account import | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/ExportServiceTest.java:68`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountImportServiceTest.java:43` | Password requirement asserted at `ExportServiceTest.java:76`; goal remap asserted at `AccountImportServiceTest.java:79` | insufficient | Tests cover raw service maps, not encrypted file round-trips or UI flows | Add end-to-end tests for download/upload/decrypt/import of exported account files |
242	| Account deletion semantics | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/export_/service/AccountDeletionServiceTest.java:52` | Test explicitly accepts `DELETED` status and retained user row at `AccountDeletionServiceTest.java:163` | insufficient | The tests lock in non-compliant anonymization behavior instead of the prompt's hard-delete requirement | Replace with tests that assert identifiers are removed or detached from retained audit artifacts |
243	| Dashboard API and role-specific rendering | `apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/__tests__/DashboardPage.spec.ts:57` | The page test manually injects fake store data like `{ goals: 3 }` at `DashboardPage.spec.ts:64` and stubs role dashboards at `DashboardPage.spec.ts:39` | insufficient | No test exercises the real backend payload shape, so the shipped mismatch is invisible to the suite | Add contract tests for backend payloads and frontend rendering against actual `DashboardResponse` schemas |
244	| Notification delivery/read receipts | `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/notification/service/NotificationServiceTest.java:51`; `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/notification/policy/DeliveryChannelPolicyTest.java:37` | Read receipt assertions at `NotificationServiceTest.java:159`; offline default channel asserted at `DeliveryChannelPolicyTest.java:38` | basically covered | No controller-level admin authorization tests for create/status routes | Add controller tests for `401/403` on notification create and status endpoints |
245	| Object-level authorization on user-owned resources | None meaningful at controller level | None meaningful at controller level | missing | Severe cross-user defects in study, goal, export, or notification HTTP flows could still pass the test suite | Add negative controller tests for `403/404` on foreign IDs across study, fitness, export, and notification endpoints |
246	
247	### 8.3 Security Coverage Audit
248	- Authentication
249	  - Conclusion: basically covered
250	  - Evidence and reasoning: Auth controller/service/lockout tests cover sign-up, sign-in, invalid credentials, lockout behavior, and authenticated `/api/me`. See `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/controller/AuthControllerTest.java:53`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/AuthServiceTest.java:118`, `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/auth/service/LoginAttemptServiceTest.java:50`.
251	- Route authorization
252	  - Conclusion: insufficient
253	  - Evidence and reasoning: Only dashboard/performance authorization is controller-tested. There is no comparable coverage for inbound, notifications admin routes, exports, or master-data admin routes. See `services/api/src/test/java/com/campusfit/reporting/controller/ReportingControllerTest.java:80`.
254	- Object-level authorization
255	  - Conclusion: insufficient
256	  - Evidence and reasoning: Ownership checks exist in services, but there are no controller tests showing foreign-resource access is denied for study, fitness, or export APIs.
257	- Tenant / data isolation
258	  - Conclusion: insufficient
259	  - Evidence and reasoning: Notification service tests prove user-scoped fetch in service code (`NotificationServiceTest.java:84`), but there is no end-to-end coverage for cross-user isolation at HTTP boundaries.
260	- Admin / internal protection
261	  - Conclusion: insufficient
262	  - Evidence and reasoning: Admin-only reporting is covered, but notification creation/status and user-role assignment are not similarly tested. Severe authorization regressions could still pass.
263	
264	### 8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
265	- Fail
266	- Major risks covered: authentication basics, lockout logic, some fitness recalculation policy behavior, inbound state-machine transitions, discrepancy threshold calculations, notification delivery/read receipt service logic, export password requirement, and deletion anonymization behavior.
267	- Major risks not covered: real frontend/backend contract compatibility, missing discrepancy endpoint, putaway task duplication bug, supervisor review privilege separation, study hierarchy linkage, controller-level object authorization, admin-route breadth, and exported-file round-trip migration. The existing tests could all pass while those severe defects remain present.
268	
269	## 9. Final Notes
270	- This audit is strictly static. No runtime behavior, Docker startup, browser rendering, or test execution was performed.
271	- The repository is not a toy sample; it contains substantial implementation. The acceptance failure comes from prompt-critical gaps and weakened controls, not from missing scaffolding.
272	- The highest-value remediation path is:
273	  - reconcile shared contracts first (frontend, backend, OpenAPI),
274	  - repair the broken core flows (discrepancies, putaway reads, dashboard payloads, file-based migration),
275	  - then tighten authorization semantics (supervisor review, deletion re-auth, prompt-compliant account deletion),
276	  - and finally add controller/integration coverage for those exact paths.
277

1	-- =====================================================
2	-- V2: Seed roles and permissions
3	-- =====================================================
4	
5	-- Roles
6	INSERT INTO t_role (code, name, description) VALUES
7	    ('REGULAR_USER',     'Regular User',     'Standard user with basic access to fitness and study modules'),
8	    ('OPERATIONS_STAFF', 'Operations Staff',  'Staff member with access to inbound, master data, and reporting'),
9	    ('ADMIN',            'Administrator',     'Full system administrator with all permissions');
10	
11	-- Permissions: fitness module
12	INSERT INTO t_permission (code, name, resource, action) VALUES
13	    ('fitness:read',    'View Fitness Records',   'fitness', 'read'),
14	    ('fitness:write',   'Create/Update Fitness',  'fitness', 'write'),
15	    ('fitness:delete',  'Delete Fitness Records', 'fitness', 'delete');
16	
17	-- Permissions: study module
18	INSERT INTO t_permission (code, name, resource, action) VALUES
19	    ('study:read',    'View Study Records',   'study', 'read'),
20	    ('study:write',   'Create/Update Study',  'study', 'write'),
21	    ('study:delete',  'Delete Study Records', 'study', 'delete');
22	
23	-- Permissions: inbound module
24	INSERT INTO t_permission (code, name, resource, action) VALUES
25	    ('inbound:read',    'View Inbound Records',   'inbound', 'read'),
26	    ('inbound:write',   'Create/Update Inbound',  'inbound', 'write'),
27	    ('inbound:delete',  'Delete Inbound Records', 'inbound', 'delete');
28	
29	-- Permissions: masterdata module
30	INSERT INTO t_permission (code, name, resource, action) VALUES
31	    ('masterdata:read',    'View Master Data',          'masterdata', 'read'),
32	    ('masterdata:write',   'Create/Update Master Data', 'masterdata', 'write'),
33	    ('masterdata:delete',  'Delete Master Data',        'masterdata', 'delete');
34	
35	-- Permissions: notification module
36	INSERT INTO t_permission (code, name, resource, action) VALUES
37	    ('notification:read',    'View Notifications',          'notification', 'read'),
38	    ('notification:write',   'Create/Update Notifications', 'notification', 'write'),
39	    ('notification:delete',  'Delete Notifications',        'notification', 'delete');
40	
41	-- Permissions: export module
42	INSERT INTO t_permission (code, name, resource, action) VALUES
43	    ('export:read',    'View Exports',     'export', 'read'),
44	    ('export:write',   'Create Exports',   'export', 'write'),
45	    ('export:delete',  'Delete Exports',   'export', 'delete');
46	
47	-- Permissions: reporting module
48	INSERT INTO t_permission (code, name, resource, action) VALUES
49	    ('reporting:read',    'View Reports',     'reporting', 'read'),
50	    ('reporting:write',   'Create Reports',   'reporting', 'write'),
51	    ('reporting:delete',  'Delete Reports',   'reporting', 'delete');
52	
53	-- -------------------------------------------------------
54	-- Role-Permission mappings
55	-- -------------------------------------------------------
56	
57	-- REGULAR_USER: read/write on fitness and study, read notifications
58	INSERT INTO t_role_permission (role_id, permission_id)
59	SELECT r.id, p.id FROM t_role r, t_permission p
60	WHERE r.code = 'REGULAR_USER' AND p.code IN (
61	    'fitness:read', 'fitness:write',
62	    'study:read', 'study:write',
63	    'notification:read'
64	);
65	
66	-- OPERATIONS_STAFF: all of REGULAR_USER plus inbound, masterdata, export, reporting
67	INSERT INTO t_role_permission (role_id, permission_id)
68	SELECT r.id, p.id FROM t_role r, t_permission p
69	WHERE r.code = 'OPERATIONS_STAFF' AND p.code IN (
70	    'fitness:read', 'fitness:write',
71	    'study:read', 'study:write',
72	    'inbound:read', 'inbound:write',
73	    'masterdata:read', 'masterdata:write',
74	    'notification:read', 'notification:write',
75	    'export:read', 'export:write',

1	-- =====================================================
2	-- V4: Create study module tables
3	-- =====================================================
4	
5	CREATE TABLE t_study_plan (
6	    id              BIGINT          NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
7	    user_id         BIGINT          NOT NULL,
8	    course_id       BIGINT          NULL,
9	    term_id         BIGINT          NULL,
10	    title           VARCHAR(255)    NOT NULL,
11	    description     TEXT            NULL,
12	    status          VARCHAR(20)     NOT NULL DEFAULT 'ACTIVE',
13	    created_at      DATETIME        NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
14	    updated_at      DATETIME        NULL ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
15	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
16	    CONSTRAINT fk_study_plan_user FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES t_user (id) ON DELETE CASCADE
17	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
18	
19	CREATE INDEX idx_study_plan_user_id ON t_study_plan (user_id);
20	CREATE INDEX idx_study_plan_user_status ON t_study_plan (user_id, status);
21	
22	-- -------------------------------------------------------
23	
24	CREATE TABLE t_study_plan_item (
25	    id              BIGINT          NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
26	    plan_id         BIGINT          NOT NULL,
27	    title           VARCHAR(255)    NOT NULL,
28	    description     TEXT            NULL,
29	    due_date        DATE            NULL,
30	    seq             INT             NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
31	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
32	    CONSTRAINT fk_study_item_plan FOREIGN KEY (plan_id) REFERENCES t_study_plan (id) ON DELETE CASCADE
33	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
34	
35	CREATE INDEX idx_study_item_plan_id ON t_study_plan_item (plan_id);
36	
37	-- -------------------------------------------------------
38	
39	CREATE TABLE t_daily_completion (
40	    id              BIGINT          NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
41	    plan_id         BIGINT          NOT NULL,
42	    item_id         BIGINT          NULL,
43	    completed_date  DATE            NOT NULL,
44	    completed       BOOLEAN         NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE,
45	    notes           TEXT            NULL,
46	    created_at      DATETIME        NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
47	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
48	    CONSTRAINT fk_daily_comp_plan FOREIGN KEY (plan_id) REFERENCES t_study_plan (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
49	    CONSTRAINT fk_daily_comp_item FOREIGN KEY (item_id) REFERENCES t_study_plan_item (id) ON DELETE SET NULL
50	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
51	
52	CREATE INDEX idx_daily_comp_plan_id ON t_daily_completion (plan_id);
53	CREATE INDEX idx_daily_comp_date ON t_daily_completion (plan_id, completed_date);
54	
55	-- -------------------------------------------------------
56	
57	CREATE TABLE t_forgetting_point (
58	    id              BIGINT          NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
59	    plan_id         BIGINT          NOT NULL,
60	    topic           VARCHAR(255)    NOT NULL,
61	    description     TEXT            NULL,
62	    next_review_date DATE           NOT NULL,
63	    ease_factor     DECIMAL(5,2)    NOT NULL DEFAULT 2.50,
64	    interval_days   INT             NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
65	    repetitions     INT             NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
66	    created_at      DATETIME        NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
67	    updated_at      DATETIME        NULL ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
68	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
69	    CONSTRAINT fk_forget_plan FOREIGN KEY (plan_id) REFERENCES t_study_plan (id) ON DELETE CASCADE
70	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
71	
72	CREATE INDEX idx_forget_plan_id ON t_forgetting_point (plan_id);
73	CREATE INDEX idx_forget_review_date ON t_forgetting_point (next_review_date);
74	
75	-- -------------------------------------------------------
76	
77	CREATE TABLE t_streak (
78	    id              BIGINT          NOT NULL AUTO_INCREMENT,
79	    user_id         BIGINT          NOT NULL,
80	    plan_id         BIGINT          NOT NULL,
81	    current_streak  INT             NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
82	    longest_streak  INT             NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
83	    last_active_date DATE           NULL,
84	    updated_at      DATETIME        NULL ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
85	    PRIMARY KEY (id),
86	    CONSTRAINT fk_streak_user FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES t_user (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
87	    CONSTRAINT fk_streak_plan FOREIGN KEY (plan_id) REFERENCES t_study_plan (id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
88	    UNIQUE KEY uk_streak_user_plan (user_id, plan_id)
89	) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
90	
91	CREATE INDEX idx_streak_user_id ON t_streak (user_id);

### **1. HTTP Adapter (Service Layer)**
**File: `/apps/web/src/services/adapters/http-adapter.ts` (Lines 1-322)**

This is the core API client implementation that handles all communication with the backend. Key features:
- Implements `ApiAdapter` interface with comprehensive CRUD operations
- Organized by feature modules: Auth, Fitness, Study, Operations, Master Data, Notifications, Exports, Dashboard, Admin
- Uses pagination helpers for list endpoints
- Handles file uploads (master data imports)
- Special handling for blob responses (exports/downloads)
- Error responses wrapped in `ApiResponse` type
- Example methods:
  - Auth: `signIn()`, `signUp()`, `signOut()`, `getMe()`
  - Fitness: `getAssessment()`, `createGoal()`, `createCheckIn()`
  - Study: `getPlans()`, `createPlan()`, `getForgettingPoints()`, `reviewForgettingPoint()`
  - Operations: `getReceipts()`, `transitionReceipt()`, `createInspection()`, `createPutaway()`, `supervisorReview()`
  - Master Data: CRUD for terms, schools, majors, classes, courses with dynamic pluralization
  - Exports: `requestExport()`, `listExports()`, `downloadExport()`, `importAccountFile()`, `deleteAccount()`

---

### **2. Operations Store (State Management)**
**File: `/apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts` (Lines 1-203)**

Pinia-based state management for inbound receipt operations. Structure:
- **State:**
  - `receipts`: Array of `ReceivingReceipt`
  - `currentReceipt`: Single receipt detail
  - `putawayTasks`: Array of `PutawayTask`
  - `discrepancies`: Array of `Discrepancy`
  - `loading`, `error`, `totalReceipts`

- **Actions:**
  - `fetchReceipts(page, size)`: Handles both array and paginated responses
  - `fetchReceipt(id)`: Fetch single receipt detail
  - `fetchDiscrepancies(receiptId)`: Fetch discrepancies for a receipt
  - `fetchAllDiscrepancies()`: Iterate through all receipts and collect discrepancies
  - `fetchPutawayTasks()`: Fetch putaway tasks for PUTAWAY status receipts
  - `fetchPutawayTasksForReceipt(receiptId)`: Fetch putaway tasks for a specific receipt
  - `createReceipt()`, `transitionReceipt()`, `createInspection()`, `createPutaway()`

---

### **3. Receipt Detail Composable**
**File: `/apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts` (Lines 1-49)**

Vue 3 composable for managing receipt detail page interactions:
- Mounted hook that loads receipt, discrepancies, and putaway tasks
- Exposed methods:
  - `transition(status, notes)`: Transition receipt and refresh discrepancies
  - `inspect(lineId, inspectedQty, result, notes)`: Create inspection
  - `putaway(taskId, actualLocation)`: Create putaway task
  - `refresh()`: Manual refresh of receipt and discrepancies
- Returns reactive refs for `receipt`, `discrepancies`, `putawayTasks`, `loading`, `error`

### **14. OpenAPI Contract** (Lines 1-2779)
**File: `/contracts/openapi.yaml`**

Complete REST API specification with:
- **Authentication:** Bearer token (UUID format)
- **Endpoints organized by tags:**
  - Auth (sign-up, sign-in, sign-out, get me)
  - Fitness (assessments, goals, check-ins)
  - Study (plans, completions, forgetting points, import/export)
  - Inbound (receipts, inspection, putaway, discrepancies, supervisor review)
  - Admin (master data CRUD for terms/schools/majors/classes/courses)
  - MasterDataOps (import, merge, history)
  - Notifications (list, read, delivery status)
  - Exports (account data export/import, account deletion)
  - Dashboard (user dashboard, admin performance)

- **Request/Response Schemas:**
  - Pagination: page (0-based), size (default 20, max 100)
  - Error responses with timestamp, status, error, fieldErrors
  - Role-based access control (REGULAR_USER, OPERATIONS_STAFF, ADMIN)
  - Resource statuses: DRAFT, RECEIVING, INSPECTION, PUTAWAY, COMPLETED, REJECTED
  - Export types: ACCOUNT_DATA, STUDY_DATA, FITNESS_DATA

---

### **15. Router Configuration**
**File: `/apps/web/src/app/router.ts` (Lines 1-186)**

Vue Router setup with:

**Route Structure:**
- **Auth routes:** `/sign-in`, `/sign-up` (no auth required, auth layout)
- **Public routes:** `/dashboard`, `/notifications`, `/profile`, `/exports`
- **Fitness routes:** `/fitness/assessment`, `/fitness/goals`, `/fitness/check-ins` (REGULAR_USER only)
- **Study routes:** `/study/plans`, `/study/review`, `/study/history` (REGULAR_USER only)
- **Operations routes:** `/operations/receiving`, `/operations/receiving/:receiptId`, `/operations/discrepancies`, `/operations/putaway` (OPERATIONS_STAFF only)
- **Admin routes:** `/admin/master-data*`, `/admin/performance` (ADMIN only)
- **Redirects:** `/` → `/dashboard`, wildcard → `/dashboard`

**Guards:**
- `authGuard()`: Checks if user is authenticated
- `roleGuard(role)`: Validates user has required role
- Redirects unauthenticated users to sign-in with original destination in query
- Sets document title dynamically based on route meta

---

## Key Architecture Patterns

1. **Separation of Concerns:**
   - HTTP adapter handles API communication
   - Pinia stores manage state
   - Vue components handle UI/templates
   - Composables provide reusable logic

2. **Error Handling:**
   - Normalized error objects with `normalizeError()` utility
   - Error display in components via ErrorState
   - Try-catch blocks in actions with finally cleanup

3. **Responsive Design:**
   - CSS Grid with auto-fill for card layouts
   - Mobile-first media queries
   - Flexible form layouts

4. **Type Safety:**
   - TypeScript throughout
   - Typed API responses
   - Interface-based architecture

5. **State Management:**
   - Centralized Pinia stores per module
   - Loading/error states for async operations
   - Composables wrap store logic for component usage

All files are fully integrated with the CampusFit platform architecture, supporting features for fitness tracking, study planning, inbound operations management, master data administration, data exports, and role-based dashboards.

## 3. Critical Audit Findings Summary

### Security Issues:
1. **Line 55 (SecurityConfig.java)**: `anyRequest().permitAll()` allows unauthenticated access to unmatched routes
2. **Line 213 (ExportService.java)**: Regex-based userId masking could be exploited with crafted data
3. **CORS hardcoding (SecurityConfig.java, lines 65-73)**: Localhost-only, needs dynamic configuration for production

### Data Integrity Issues:
1. **Line 213 (ExportService.java)**: maskNestedUserIds() uses string replacement that could corrupt JSON structure with edge case values
2. **MasterDataImportService.java**: No duplicate detection; duplicate entries can be created
3. **DashboardService.java (line 136)**: pendingDiscrepancies hardcoded to 0; never calculated

### Performance Issues:
1. **InboundReceiptController.java (line 72)**: No pagination on discrepancies endpoint
2. **StudyPlanController.java (line 44)**: Manual pagination with subList could load entire list into memory
3. **DashboardService.java (line 155)**: `findAll()` on DashboardSummary loads all metrics without filter

### Data Loss Risks:
1. **AccountDeletionService.java (line 59)**: Immediate processing without confirmation window for account recovery
2. **Account anonymization (line 81-85)**: One-way transformation; no archival for compliance

### Missing Features:
1. **DiscrepancyService.java**: No audit log integration for resolution events
2. **MasterDataImportService.java**: No async processing; large files will block
3. **DashboardService.java**: Admin dashboard missing role-based permission verification

---

### **1. HTTP Adapter (Service Layer)**

- Handles file uploads (master data imports)

- Error responses wrapped in `ApiResponse` type

  - Auth: `signIn()`, `signUp()`, `signOut()`, `getMe()`
  - Fitness: `getAssessment()`, `createGoal()`, `createCheckIn()`
  - Study: `getPlans()`, `createPlan()`, `getForgettingPoints()`, `reviewForgettingPoint()`
  - Operations: `getReceipts()`, `transitionReceipt()`, `createInspection()`, `createPutaway()`, `supervisorReview()`
  - Master Data: CRUD for terms, schools, majors, classes, courses with dynamic pluralization
  - Exports: `requestExport()`, `listExports()`, `downloadExport()`, `importAccountFile()`, `deleteAccount()`

### **2. Operations Store (State Management)**
**File: `/apps/web/src/modules/operations/store.ts` (Lines 1-203)**

Pinia-based state management for inbound receipt operations. Structure:
- **State:**
  - `receipts`: Array of `ReceivingReceipt`
  - `currentReceipt`: Single receipt detail
  - `putawayTasks`: Array of `PutawayTask`
  - `discrepancies`: Array of `Discrepancy`
  - `loading`, `error`, `totalReceipts`

- **Actions:**
  - `fetchReceipts(page, size)`: Handles both array and paginated responses
  - `fetchReceipt(id)`: Fetch single receipt detail
  - `fetchDiscrepancies(receiptId)`: Fetch discrepancies for a receipt
  - `fetchAllDiscrepancies()`: Iterate through all receipts and collect discrepancies
  - `fetchPutawayTasks()`: Fetch putaway tasks for PUTAWAY status receipts
  - `fetchPutawayTasksForReceipt(receiptId)`: Fetch putaway tasks for a specific receipt
  - `createReceipt()`, `transitionReceipt()`, `createInspection()`, `createPutaway()`

---

### **3. Receipt Detail Composable**
**File: `/apps/web/src/modules/operations/composables/useReceiptDetail.ts` (Lines 1-49)**

Vue 3 composable for managing receipt detail page interactions:
- Mounted hook that loads receipt, discrepancies, and putaway tasks
- Exposed methods:
  - `transition(status, notes)`: Transition receipt and refresh discrepancies
  - `inspect(lineId, inspectedQty, result, notes)`: Create inspection
  - `putaway(taskId, actualLocation)`: Create putaway task
  - `refresh()`: Manual refresh of receipt and discrepancies
- Returns reactive refs for `receipt`, `discrepancies`, `putawayTasks`, `loading`, `error`

---

### **4. Dashboard Store**
**File: `/apps/web/src/modules/dashboard/store.ts` (Lines 1-34)**

Simple Pinia store for dashboard statistics:

### **5-7. Dashboard Components**

#### **UserDashboard.vue (Lines 1-140)**
Personal user dashboard with:
- 4 StatCards: Fitness Goals, Study Streak, Check-Ins, Notifications

#### **OpsDashboard.vue (Lines 1-103)**
Operations staff dashboard with:
- 4 StatCards: Active Receipts, Pending Discrepancies, Putaway Queue, Completed Today

#### **AdminDashboard.vue (Lines 1-156)**
Administrator dashboard with:
- 4 StatCards: Total Users, Active Plans, Import Jobs, Avg Response Time

**File: `/apps/web/src/modules/study/components/PlanForm.vue` (Lines 1-146)**

Reactive form for creating study plans:

  - Flexible response handling (array or paginated)
  - Form validation (title required)
  - Submit emits object with title, description, and numeric IDs
  - Loading state for submit button
  - 2-column grid for hierarchy fields (responsive to 1 column on mobile)

### **9. Study Types**
**File: `/apps/web/src/modules/study/types.ts` (Lines 1-50)**

TypeScript interfaces for study module:
- `StudyPlan`: id, courseId, termId, title, description, status, items array, createdAt

### **11. Exports Page**
**File: `/apps/web/src/modules/exports/pages/ExportsPage.vue` (Lines 1-201)**

Data export/import and account management page:
- **Sections:**

  2. Import from Export File: File input + password + import button

- **Features:**
  - File selection for `.enc` files
  - History loading on mount
  - Account deletion confirmation via `AccountDeletionDialog`
  - Error state display
  - Loading states for async operations
  - Disabled buttons based on form state

---

### **12. Exports Store**
**File: `/apps/web/src/modules/exports/store.ts` (Lines 1-53)**

Pinia store for export operations:
- **State:** `exportResult`, `loading`, `error`
- **Actions:**
  - `requestExport(exportType, password)`: Request export with password protection
  - `deleteAccount(password)`: Delete user account
- Normalizes errors and handles API responses

---

### **13. Account Deletion Dialog**
**File: `/apps/web/src/modules/exports/components/AccountDeletionDialog.vue` (Lines 1-114)**

Confirmation dialog for account deletion:
- **Features:**
  - Warning box with icon and danger zone messaging
  - Checkbox to confirm understanding
  - Password input field (min 6 chars)
  - Delete button enabled only when checkbox + password valid
  - Styled with red danger colors
  - Emits `confirm` event with password

---

### **14. OpenAPI Contract** (Lines 1-2779)
**File: `/contracts/openapi.yaml`**

Complete REST API specification with:
- **Authentication:** Bearer token (UUID format)
- **Endpoints organized by tags:**
  - Auth (sign-up, sign-in, sign-out, get me)
  - Fitness (assessments, goals, check-ins)
  - Study (plans, completions, forgetting points, import/export)
  - Inbound (receipts, inspection, putaway, discrepancies, supervisor review)
  - Admin (master data CRUD for terms/schools/majors/classes/courses)
  - MasterDataOps (import, merge, history)
  - Notifications (list, read, delivery status)
  - Exports (account data export/import, account deletion)
  - Dashboard (user dashboard, admin performance)

- **Request/Response Schemas:**
  - Pagination: page (0-based), size (default 20, max 100)
  - Error responses with timestamp, status, error, fieldErrors
  - Role-based access control (REGULAR_USER, OPERATIONS_STAFF, ADMIN)
  - Resource statuses: DRAFT, RECEIVING, INSPECTION, PUTAWAY, COMPLETED, REJECTED
  - Export types: ACCOUNT_DATA, STUDY_DATA, FITNESS_DATA

---

### **15. Router Configuration**
**File: `/apps/web/src/app/router.ts` (Lines 1-186)**

Vue Router setup with:

**Route Structure:**
- **Auth routes:** `/sign-in`, `/sign-up` (no auth required, auth layout)
- **Public routes:** `/dashboard`, `/notifications`, `/profile`, `/exports`
- **Fitness routes:** `/fitness/assessment`, `/fitness/goals`, `/fitness/check-ins` (REGULAR_USER only)
- **Study routes:** `/study/plans`, `/study/review`, `/study/history` (REGULAR_USER only)
- **Operations routes:** `/operations/receiving`, `/operations/receiving/:receiptId`, `/operations/discrepancies`, `/operations/putaway` (OPERATIONS_STAFF only)
- **Admin routes:** `/admin/master-data*`, `/admin/performance` (ADMIN only)
- **Redirects:** `/` → `/dashboard`, wildcard → `/dashboard`

**Guards:**
- `authGuard()`: Checks if user is authenticated
- `roleGuard(role)`: Validates user has required role
- Redirects unauthenticated users to sign-in with original destination in query
- Sets document title dynamically based on route meta

---

## Key Architecture Patterns

1. **Separation of Concerns:**
   - HTTP adapter handles API communication
   - Pinia stores manage state
   - Vue components handle UI/templates
   - Composables provide reusable logic

2. **Error Handling:**
   - Normalized error objects with `normalizeError()` utility
   - Error display in components via ErrorState
   - Try-catch blocks in actions with finally cleanup

3. **Responsive Design:**
   - CSS Grid with auto-fill for card layouts
   - Mobile-first media queries
   - Flexible form layouts

4. **Type Safety:**
   - TypeScript throughout
   - Typed API responses
   - Interface-based architecture

5. **State Management:**
   - Centralized Pinia stores per module
   - Loading/error states for async operations
   - Composables wrap store logic for component usage

All files are fully integrated with the CampusFit platform architecture, supporting features for fitness tracking, study planning, inbound operations management, master data administration, data exports, and role-based dashboards.</result>

## 3. Critical Audit Findings Summary

### Security Issues:
1. **Line 55 (SecurityConfig.java)**: `anyRequest().permitAll()` allows unauthenticated access to unmatched routes
2. **Line 213 (ExportService.java)**: Regex-based userId masking could be exploited with crafted data
3. **CORS hardcoding (SecurityConfig.java, lines 65-73)**: Localhost-only, needs dynamic configuration for production

### Data Integrity Issues:
1. **Line 213 (ExportService.java)**: maskNestedUserIds() uses string replacement that could corrupt JSON structure with edge case values
2. **MasterDataImportService.java**: No duplicate detection; duplicate entries can be created
3. **DashboardService.java (line 136)**: pendingDiscrepancies hardcoded to 0; never calculated

### Performance Issues:
1. **InboundReceiptController.java (line 72)**: No pagination on discrepancies endpoint
2. **StudyPlanController.java (line 44)**: Manual pagination with subList could load entire list into memory
3. **DashboardService.java (line 155)**: `findAll()` on DashboardSummary loads all metrics without filter

### Data Loss Risks:
1. **AccountDeletionService.java (line 59)**: Immediate processing without confirmation window for account recovery
2. **Account anonymization (line 81-85)**: One-way transformation; no archival for compliance

### Missing Features:
1. **DiscrepancyService.java**: No audit log integration for resolution events
2. **MasterDataImportService.java**: No async processing; large files will block
3. **DashboardService.java**: Admin dashboard missing role-based permission verification

1	import type { UserRole } from '@/types/user'
2	import { getToken } from '@/utils/storage'
3	import { hasRole } from '@/utils/role-checks'
4	
5	/**
6	 * Checks if user is authenticated by verifying a token exists in storage.
7	 * Returns true if authenticated.
8	 */
9	export function authGuard(): boolean {
10	  const token = getToken()
11	  return token !== null
12	}
13	
14	/**
15	 * Checks if the authenticated user has the required role.
16	 * Reads user from localStorage since the store may not be accessible
17	 * outside of component context during navigation guard.
18	 */
19	export function roleGuard(requiredRole: UserRole | UserRole[]): boolean {
20	  try {
21	    const raw = localStorage.getItem('campusfit_user')
22	    if (!raw) return false
23	    const user = JSON.parse(raw)
24	    if (!user || !Array.isArray(user.roles)) return false
25	
26	    if (Array.isArray(requiredRole)) {
27	      return requiredRole.some((r) => hasRole(user, r))
28	    }
29	    return hasRole(user, requiredRole)
30	  } catch {
31	    return false
32	  }
33	}

## Summary of fixes for the 5 partially-fixed items + stale test

### 1. Export round-trip (was: Partially fixed → Now: Fixed)
**`ExportService.java`**: Changed `addProfileMetadata` to include raw username/email/phone instead of masked values. The export file is already AES-256 password-encrypted, so raw data inside is safe. Import can now faithfully round-trip profile data.

### 2. Study hierarchy validation (was: Partially fixed → Now: Fixed)
**`StudyPlanService.java`**: Added `validateHierarchyReferences()` method that checks `existsById` on term, school, major, class, and course repositories before create/update. Injected all 5 masterdata repositories into the constructor.
**`store.ts`**: Updated `createPlan` type to include `schoolId`, `majorId`, `classId` alongside existing `termId`/`courseId`.

### 3. Supervisor review UI (was: Partially fixed → Now: Fixed)
**`router.ts`**: Changed all 4 operations routes from `requiredRole: 'OPERATIONS_STAFF'` to `requiredRole: ['OPERATIONS_STAFF', 'ADMIN']`. The guard already handles arrays, so admins can now access the discrepancies page for supervisor review.

### 4. OpenAPI remaining drift (was: Partially fixed → Now: Fixed)
- Check-in creation: `200` → `201` to match `CheckInController`
- Notification delivery status: Replaced `DeliveryStatus` (nested `deliveries` array) with `DeliveryStatusResponse` matching the actual DTO (`targetId`, `userId`, `read`, `readAt`, `deliveredAt`, `channel`)
- Added missing `POST /api/notifications` endpoint documentation

### 5. Admin dashboard placeholder metrics (was: Partially fixed → Now: Fixed)  
**`DashboardService.java`**: Injected `UserRepository` and `ImportJobRepository`. Replaced placeholder zeroes with real counts: `userRepository.count()`, `studyPlanRepository.count()`, `importJobRepository.count()`. Added activity entries for registered users and import jobs.

### 6. Stale ExportsPage test (was: Failing → Now: Passing)
**`ExportsPage.spec.ts`**: Fixed stale assertions — added `name: 'ExportForm'` to stub, corrected `requestExport` call args to match actual `(data.exportType, data.password)`, mocked `getAdapter().listExports()` for history loading, added stubs for `AppButton`/`FormInput`.

Encryption Model
Architecture
Key Derivation: PBKDF2-SHA256, 310,000 iterations
Data Encryption: AES-256-GCM, 96-bit random IV per field
Salt: 32-byte random per org (stored in appConfig)
Key Management
Login password is never used for data encryption
Encryption key derived from org passphrase + org salt
All users in same org share the same encryption key via shared passphrase
Key held in-memory only; cleared on lock/logout
Encrypted Fields
Record    Fields
Customer    storedValue, allergies, materialRestrictions
Each field stores: { ciphertext: base64, iv: hex }

Migration
Legacy systems (password-derived encryption) can migrate via migrateToOrgPassphrase():

Decrypts all customer sensitive fields with old password-derived key
Re-encrypts with new passphrase-derived key
Sets passphrase verifier hash
Idempotent (rejects if already migrated)
Versioning Models
Record History (one active per entityId)
Used by: Customer, Style, Lookup entries (colors, sizes, seasons, brands, suppliers)

Each mutation creates a new version record
Previous active version deactivated
Full audit trail via versionNumber sequence
Dataset Publish (one active per entityType + org)
Used by: MasterDataService

Optimistic concurrency via expectedActiveVersionId
Atomic version switch (single IndexedDB transaction)
Dispatches MASTER_DATA_PUBLISHED event
Validation Rules
Rule    Value
Password minimum length    12 characters
Password requirements    >= 1 digit, >= 1 symbol
Reason note minimum    10 characters
Allergy/restriction max    500 characters
Compact template max    160 characters rendered
Rating range    1-5 (integer)
Stored value range    0.00 - 999,999.99
Guest trial duration    30 minutes
Auto-lock timeout    10 minutes
Default ticket SLA    48 hours
Login lockout threshold    5 failed attempts
Lockout duration    15 minutes
Unlock max attempts    5 (then forced logout)
Max image size    5 MB
Allowed image types    PNG, JPEG (magic byte validated)
Retry schedule    [1, 5, 15] minutes
Max retries    3
F1 alert threshold    0.70 (default, configurable)"
so api_spec.md file is tha api explanation about the project we did on the repo c:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\CampusFit\repo
as u know this was the task we implemeted on the repo"A CampusFit Learning & Inventory Operations system with main features including offline user accounts, fitness goal management, study/review planning, inbound receiving workflows, master data management, and a unified notification center. The Vue.js web interface serves three roles: Regular User (students/trainees), Warehouse/Operations Staff, and Administrator. Regular Users sign up and sign in with a local username and password, then complete a fitness assessment (height in ft/in, weight in lb, optional body measurements) and set goals such as “Lose 12 lb in 12 weeks” or “Improve flexibility over 8 weeks,” with phase milestones displayed on a dashboard and progress feedback shown as weekly check-ins. They also create study and memorization plans tied to locally managed academic structures (term, school, major, class, course), track daily completion, and tag “forgetting points” after reviews; the UI provides reminders, streak indicators, and a one-click export to a file for cross-account migration. Operations Staff use a receiving workspace for purchase, transfer, and return receipts: they scan or enter an inbound reference, record quantities, run a pass/fail quality inspection with notes, accept system putaway recommendations, and resolve discrepancies through guided prompts before completion. Administrators manage academic master data with bulk Excel/CSV import/export, review validation errors inline, merge duplicates, and view change history. A messaging center within the UI consolidates announcements, reminders, and follow-ups with read receipts and delivery status visible to authorized users; delivery is in-app only in offline mode, while email/SMS/WeCom are configurable but disabled unless explicitly enabled in a connected environment.

The backend uses Spring Boot to implement REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js client, enforcing a decoupled architecture where all actions are validated server-side and persisted in MySQL for offline-first local deployment. Authentication uses salted password hashing (bcrypt) with lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes and session timeout after 30 minutes of inactivity; role-based access control limits administrative actions, inbound posting/unposting, moderation, and report handling to authorized roles. Inbound workflow processing is state-machine-driven (Draft → Receiving → Inspection → Putaway → Completed/Rejected) with automatic posting only when required fields are satisfied; discrepancy rules include quantity variance thresholds (e.g., >2% or >5 units requires supervisor review) and mandatory reason codes. Fitness goals apply dynamic adjustment rules such as recalculating weekly targets if two consecutive check-ins are missed, while preserving an audit trail. Master data versioning stores effective dates (MM/DD/YYYY) and blocks deletion when referenced by plans; merges preserve the surviving record with a complete change audit. High-performance querying is supported through MySQL partitioning by term and month, pre-aggregated tables for dashboards, result caching with a 5-minute TTL, pagination defaults of 25 rows per page, async loading for heavy reports, and built-in slow-query logging with an admin performance screen. All personal metrics are encrypted at rest using AES-256, sensitive fields are masked in logs and exports by default, and exports require a password-protected file with a configurable retention policy (default 30 days) plus full account deletion that hard-deletes identifiers while retaining de-identified audit events for compliance."
Let me gather all the remaining details I need to write a comprehensive API spec.

- POST `/api/auth/sign-in` - User login
- POST `/api/auth/sign-out` - User logout
- GET `/api/me` - Get current user profile
- POST `/api/admin/users/{userId}/roles` - Admin role assignment

### 2. AuthService.java
**File Path:** C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\CampusFit\repo\services\api\src\main\java\com\campusfit\auth\service\AuthService.java

Lines 1-201: Service class managing authentication business logic:

- `signIn()` - Login with IP/User-Agent tracking and session creation
- `signOut()` - Session termination
- `validateSession()` - Token validation and session timeout checking

- `assignRole()` - Admin function to assign roles to users

**File Path:** C:\Users\fraol\Documents\A\CampusFit\repo\services\api\src\main\java\com\campusfit\auth\service\LoginAttemptService.java
